motion.$description: Tier-1 motion primitives — raw durations + cubic-bezier easings. Theme- and brand-agnostic (motion timing does not change per brand or theme). Semantic motion tokens alias these by intent (`semantic.motion.duration.base` → `{motion.duration.260}`). (Easings moved to the semantic tier — they were intent-named, not descriptive, and clashed with the same names at semantic. See semantic/foundations.tokens.json.)

duration.$description: Raw timing values on a perceptual cadence: 100/180/260/400/600 ms. Step parity is roughly 1.6× — fast enough to read as crisp, slow enough to be perceived as deliberate. Anything <100ms reads as instant; anything >600ms reads as sluggish in product UI.
